description:
You are an automation specialist preparing a minimal CI prototype inside the
project directory /home/user/projects/ci.

Your goal is to:

1. Update the YAML file /home/user/projects/ci/pipeline.yml so it **exactly** matches
   the text shown below (indentation is two spaces, no trailing spaces):

version: "1.0"
jobs:
  build:
    steps:
      - run: echo "Building"
  test:
    needs: ["build"]
    steps:
      - run: pytest -q

2. Create the TOML file /home/user/projects/ci/config/vars.toml with the following
   **exact** contents (every line is terminated by a Unix newline, and there is a
   newline after the last line):

[default]
python_version = "3.11"
timeout        = 300

3. Create the plain-text log file /home/user/projects/ci/changes.log that contains
   exactly these two lines, in the order shown, each terminated by a Unix newline
   (no extra blank lines and no trailing spaces):

ADDED test job to pipeline.yml
CREATED config/vars.toml

Style / verification requirements:

• All three files must be world-readable (chmod 644 or more permissive).  
• The directory /home/user/projects/ci/config/ must exist after you finish.  
• The YAML, TOML and log files must each end with a single newline character.  
• The agent should use at most 2–3 shell commands in total to accomplish the task.  
• Do not include any explanatory output in the log file; only the two lines shown above.

When you are finished, the automated checker will read the three files and
verify that they match the exact byte-for-byte content shown here and that the
permissions are correct.
truth:

   The sub-directory /home/user/projects/ci/config/ does NOT exist.

2. Contents of the pre-existing /home/user/projects/ci/pipeline.yml:

version: "1.0"
jobs:
  build:
    steps:
      - run: echo "Building"

Desired final state after the agent completes:

---------------------------------------------------
